content.full_text:
December 11, 1979


Mr. J. Tilman Williams
Satellite Financial Advisors
12311 Harbor Blvd.
P.O. Box 1538
Garden Grove, California 92642

Dear Mr. Williams:

Thank you for your letter of December 6, 1979, requesting
clarification of my advice to you on November 19.

The answer to your most recent question is no.

The paragraph of my letter to which you refer does not concern
income received by you for the performance of services in the course
of your business. My letter refers instead to money spent by you,
your business or raised from others to pay the costs of preparing,
producing or distributing the circulars, leaflets, cards and other
items that you distribute for political purposes or include in mail-
ings sent for political purposes.

If, for example you received contributions from others to pay
the cost of printing, producing or distributing the copies of the
"Free Market Evaluation" certificate to be included in political
mailings, the funds so received would be reportable contributions.
The proceeds from a real estate sale resulting from the presentation
of one of the certificates so distributed would not be a reportable
item.

I hope this information answers your question. If you need
further assistance, please call at 916/322-5562.

Sincerely,


John Giansello
Political Reform Analyst

SATELLITE FINANCIAL ADVISORS
12311 Harbor blvd. • P.O. Box 1536 • 1¼ Mile South of Disneyland
GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642
Phone: (714) 750-4333

DECEMBER 6, 1979

STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. BOX 807
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95804

ATTENTION: JOHN GIANSELLO, Political Reform Analyst

REFERENCE: YOUR LETTER DATED November 19, 1979

I am not quite clear on your first paragraph on page two of
your letter.

Do you mean that if a person phones my office and wants a free
market evaluation of his property and after I give him an
evaluation he decides to list his property with me and I sell
his property. Do you mean that I have to report that income
as a contribution to the State?

Thank you for your prompt attention.

Sincerely,

Tilman Williams, db

November 19, 1979


Mr. J. Tilman Williams
Satellite Financial Advisors
12311 Harbor Blvd.
P.O. Box 1538
Garden Grove, CA 92642

Dear Mr. Williams:

In your letter of November 5, 1979, you have enclosed several
items you distribute in connection with both your business and
your coming campaign for city office. You ask substantially
whether these materials comply with the law.

It is possible for us to answer your question only with
respect to the law we administer, the Political Reform Act of 1974.
Other provisions of law, particularly the Elections Code, may also
be relevant to your questions. You may contact the Secretary of
State for advice on the Elections Code.

The Political Reform Act ("the Act") does not regulate the
content of political messages. Therefore, the language, format
and layout of the materials you have supplied would not violate
the Act.

By the terms of the Act, however, you are a candidate (see
Govt. Code Section 82007), and all payments made by you will be
reportable expenditures "unless it is clear from surrounding
circumstances that the payment was made for personal purposes
unrelated to [your candidacy]" (see 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section
18225(b)(1)).

Items 1, 3 and 4 of your materials clearly are distributed
for political purposes in that they either expressly acknowledge
their political character or ask people to vote for you.

Item number 2, the "Free Market Evaluation" certificate,
insofar as it is distributed solely in connection with your
business would not be a reportable item. To the extent it is,
however, included in materials that are distributed for political
purposes, the cost of the item would be a reportable expenditure.

Mr. J. Tilman Williams                 -2-                 November 19, 1979


     Any money spent by you or your business or raised from others
to pay the costs of preparation, production or distribution of the
items distributed for political purposes would be reportable as
contributions received from you, your business or others, respec-
tively.

     I hope these remarks are helpful to you.  If you have further
questions, you may call me at 916/222-5662.

                                        Sincerely yours,


                                        John Giansello
                                        Political Reform Analyst

SATELLITE FINANCIAL ADVISORS
12311 Harbor Blvd. • P.O. Box 1538 • 1¼ Mile South of Disneyland
GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642
Phone: (714) 750-4333

NOVEMBER 5, 1979

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE & ANALYSIS DIVISION
1100 KAY STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814


Gentlemen:

It is my intention to run for Mayor or City Council of the
City of Garden Grove next April 3, 1980.

Enclosed please find four items marked #1, #2, #3, and #4.

In order to comply with the law I would like your opinion
in regard to items #1 & #2 at this time. I have always tried
to do two, three or four things at the same time thus saving
time, conserving energy and practicing conservation. I am
a Real Estate Broker, Investor, Insurance Agent and I will be
a candidate next year for Mayor or City Council. (I haven't
decided)

My office has been sending out items #1 & #2 and I have been
getting a good response in regard to the item #2. I have
listed two homes, wrote some insurance and have had calls
from people asking me what I am running for. My answer is
"I don't know - either Mayor or City Council"

I intend to have 25 to 50 item #3 printed or painted on
4 x 8 plywood. Is the wording on the item #3 OK? I am not
on the City Council at the present time but I was Mayor from
1976 77 78. I want to use the word "RETURN" instead of
Elect. Is that OK?

I intend to use my business envelope item #4 with the words
"ALSO POLITICAL MATERIAL ENCLOSED" stamped on the envelope.
Is that OK?

Thank you for for your prompt attention.

Sincerely

J. Tilman Williams, db
Financial Advisor

SATELLITE FINANCIAL ADVISORS
12311 Harbor Blvd. • P.O. Box 1538 • 1¼ Mile South of Disneyland
GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642
Phone: (714) 750-4333


HI NEIGHBOR:


I want to THANK YOU for letting me serve as your Mayor from
1976 - 77 - 78. I really enjoyed working with the people of
Garden Grove and the City Staff. As you will recall, while
I was in office I returned over $340,000.00 to the Homeowners
plus created over 7,000 Local Jobs in the private sector.

I want you to be the first to know that I will be running
again in 1980. I want your vote once again.

Enclosed please find Certificate for a FREE MARKET EVALUATION
of your Home or income property. You will want this Certificate when the time comes when you decide to sell your Home or
make investments to keep ahead of Inflation, so please keep
it in a safe place.

I want you to feel free to phone me or drop by my office when
you have a question in regard to Real Estate, Investments,
Insurance or just to say hello. Lets you and I work together
as a team.

Sincerely

J Tilman Williams
J. Tilman Williams
Financial Advisor

FREE MARKET EVALUATION
Certificate

The peace of mind is pride[unclear] or preserved soul up by

STREETNAME RANCH, Arizona & Plainview

Dedicated with Dedication

JILLIMAN WILLIAMS
2211 Hilltop Boulevard
P.O. Box 1388
Glendale, CA 90262
(714) 504-4333

Associated with Stellar Realty & Investments, Stellar Mortgage & Loan Consultants, E.H. Covad Shelter Insurance Agency

[Note: The document appears to be a decorative certificate with an ornate wavy border frame. The text is oriented vertically/sideways in the image. There are two compass rose symbols - one in the upper center and one in the lower center of the certificate.]

If You Like What You See
Vote For Me
U 11 MAN WILLAMIS

CIVIC ACTIVITIES

1. Most of Guines Good 1968-1978
2. Rumored Cars $400 to $2,000 local
3. Called Member Rabes 1981 in Grove
4. General Membership of Civic Groups
5. Williams a Mayor for real over 7,000 local
   residents and businesses.

DEDICATED TO SERVE MY FELLOW MAN AS A PUBLIC SERVANT

#43

SATELLITE FINAL ADVISORS

CARIBAS GROVE CAIRNRA 94242

[illegible signature/mark]

POLITICAL SCIENTIST ENCLOSED

HAJ HARPER BUILO • CABO 1538

HAJ HARPER BUILD • CABO 1538

ELECT
DEPENDENT

HONEST

J. TILMAN WILLIAMS
SATELLITE
FINANCIAL ADVISOR

INSURANCE - REAL ESTATE - INVESTMENTS

12311 Harbor Boulevard 1½ Miles South of Disneyland
Garden Grove, California 92640

(714) 750-4333

sections.question: Do you mean that if a person phones my office and wants a free market evaluation of his property and after I give him an evaluation he decides to list his property with me and I sell his property. Do you mean that I have to report that income as a contribution to the State?
sections.conclusion: The answer to your most recent question is no. The paragraph of my letter to which you refer does not concern income received by you for the performance of services in the course of your business. My letter refers instead to money spent by you, your business or raised from others to pay the costs of preparing, producing or distributing the circulars, leaflets, cards and other items that you distribute for political purposes or include in mailings sent for political purposes. If, for example you received contributions from others to pay the cost of printing, producing or distributing the copies of the "Free Market Evaluation" certificate to be included in political mailings, the funds so received would be reportable contributions. The proceeds from a real estate sale resulting from the presentation of one of the certificates so distributed would not be a reportable item.
sections.question_synthetic: Mr. Williams seeks clarification on whether income from real estate sales resulting from free market evaluations offered to political supporters must be reported as contributions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that income from real estate sales is not reportable as a contribution, but funds received from others to pay for production and distribution of political materials (including the Free Market Evaluation certificates) would be reportable contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letters in sequence (November 5 initial request, November 19 FPPC response, December 6 follow-up request, December 11 FPPC clarification). Extracted primary structure from the final December 11 advice letter. Earlier correspondence provides context for the question. All referenced materials (certificates, flyers, business cards) are described in document.

QUESTION: Do you mean that if a person phones my office and wants a free market evaluation of his property and after I give him an evaluation he decides to list his property with me and I sell his property. Do you mean that I have to report that income as a contribution to the State?

CONCLUSION: The answer to your most recent question is no. The paragraph of my letter to which you refer does not concern income received by you for the performance of services in the course of your business. My letter refers instead to money spent by you, your business or raised from others to pay the costs of preparing, producing or distributing the circulars, leaflets, cards and other items that you distribute for political purposes or include in mailings sent for political purposes. If, for example you received contributions from others to pay the cost of printing, producing or distributing the copies of the "Free Market Evaluation" certificate to be included in political mailings, the funds so received would be reportable contributions. The proceeds from a real estate sale resulting from the presentation of one of the certificates so distributed would not be a reportable item.

embedding.summary: This is FPPC's follow-up advice letter clarifying that real estate sales proceeds are not reportable contributions under the Political Reform Act, but funds spent or raised for producing and distributing political materials are reportable.
